PHASE: Phase 7 active — tenth Feature Update merged. Phase 7 #10 (socket-client — client-side Socket.IO provider hook) squash-merged 2026-05-17 as `9d21461`. NEW pure factory `apps/web/src/lib/socket/client.ts` wraps `socket.io-client`'s `io()` with `withCredentials:true` so the Auth.js cookie flows cross-origin from APP_PORT=43512 to SOCKET_PORT=43515. NEW pure handler `apps/web/src/lib/socket/session-invalidation.ts` subscribes to the server-emitted `session:invalidated` event from the Phase 7 #8e-2 revalidation loop and returns a disposer. NEW `apps/web/src/lib/socket/socket-context.tsx` ("use client") composes the factory + handler with `useRouter().push("/login") + .refresh()` as the invalidation callback; exports `useSocket()` and `useSocketOptional()`. Wired into `apps/web/src/app/app/layout.tsx`. The Provider stays thin because Yelli's vitest env is `node`-only (no jsdom, no @testing-library/react) — testable behavior lives in the two pure modules (10 RED→GREEN tests). ALSO FIXED two pre-existing regressions caught by Phase 7 #10's `pnpm build` validation: (a) Phase 7 #8e build break — webpack statically analyses dynamic-import string literals in instrumentation.ts for BOTH Node AND Edge graphs regardless of runtime gates; the Edge graph has no `http`/`crypto`/`path` so `pnpm build` failed silently for every commit since #8e; fixed by Edge-runtime webpack alias in next.config.ts. (b) Phase 7 #9 .npmrc-threshold false claim — pnpm 10's `pnpm audit` does NOT honor `.npmrc audit-level` for its exit code (only `pnpm config get audit-level` reads it); CI was silently broken since #9; restored `--audit-level=critical` CLI flag in ci.yml. Test suite 75→85 (+10). 3 new lessons logged: 🔴 [[pnpm10-audit-level-ignored]], 🔴 [[instrumentation-edge-stub-required]], 🟤 (socket-client provider pattern — pure factory + pure handler + thin React shell, extends [[pure-helper-extraction-pattern]] to React-incompatible test environments).

LESSONS_ADDED (3 new entries):
  🔴 [[pnpm10-audit-level-ignored]] — pnpm 10's `pnpm audit` does NOT honor `.npmrc audit-level` for exit code; only the explicit `--audit-level=<level>` CLI flag controls the exit code. `pnpm config get audit-level` DOES read the .npmrc (so config introspection works), but the audit command itself does not. Phase 7 #9 was caught by Phase 7 #10's `pnpm build` validation. Rule: declare the policy in BOTH .npmrc (documentation) AND the CI command line (enforcement); keep them in sync.
  🔴 [[instrumentation-edge-stub-required]] — Next.js 15 instrumentation hook needs Edge-runtime webpack stub for Node-only deps despite dynamic-import gates. Webpack statically analyses string-literal `await import("...")` chunks for BOTH Node AND Edge graphs regardless of any runtime `process.env.NEXT_RUNTIME !== "nodejs"` early-return. Fix: in next.config.ts `webpack:` add `config.resolve.alias = { ..., "socket.io": false, "@/server/socket/server": false, "@/server/socket/revalidation": false }` and `config.resolve.fallback = { http: false, https: false, crypto: false, path: false }` for the edge runtime. `serverExternalPackages` does NOT help here — it applies to Server Components, not the instrumentation Edge chunk. Discipline: every ticket touching instrumentation.ts MUST validate with `pnpm build`, not just test/typecheck/lint.
  🟤 (socket-client provider pattern) — pure factory + pure handler + thin React shell. The Yelli vitest config is `environment: "node"` and jsdom is NOT installed; React component tests with `@testing-library/react` are impossible. Factor the testable behavior into pure modules (factory + event-handler) so RED→GREEN runs in node-env. The Provider becomes a thin glue layer (factory + handler + useRouter) shielded by typecheck + lint + Visual QA. Extends [[pure-helper-extraction-pattern]] (Phase 7 #7c-2 + #8e — separate-file extraction of Edge-incompatible logic) to React-incompatible test environments. Reusable pattern for any Context provider where the testable behavior can be lifted into a non-React module.

TIER_CLASSIFICATION: 1 — lightweight, with documented scope additions. Core ticket: 7 files (5 new + 2 modified — types + layout) within `apps/web/src/lib/socket/` + `apps/web/src/app/app/layout.tsx`, single module, dependency depth 1. Scope additions: 2 pre-existing regression fixes (next.config.ts + ci.yml) — orthogonal but both required for honest Rule 25 two-stage review pass. Per memory-governance §1 Step 1 + Step 2: ≤4 files in the core ticket + low coupling → Tier 1; the scope additions raised the actual touch count to 9 but added no new dependency depth. No Sonnet dispatch, no §2.5b escalation justification needed (~30K total Opus context — comfortably under 80K SAFE zone).

PHASE_7_TICKET_10_NOTES:
  - Why fix the two pre-existing regressions in scope: Rule 25 two-stage review requires the build + audit gates to pass for an honest "complete" claim. Both gates were silently broken on `main` (build since #8e, audit since #9) and STATE.md from those tickets carried stale "27 routes — unchanged" + "audit exit 0" claims without re-verification. Phase 7 #10's `pnpm build` was the first validation that re-ran them; the surface area to fix (one webpack alias + one CLI flag) was 1-2 lines each. Letting them ship broken would have set a precedent that "validation gates can be skipped for routine work". The build fix is structurally identical to the existing `serverExternalPackages: ["isomorphic-dompurify", "jsdom"]` line — same precedent.
  - Why factor into 3 modules instead of one Provider: vitest env is node-only (jsdom not installed). The pure factory + pure handler are 100% testable in node; the Provider is unitarily-testable only with jsdom + RTL. Splitting moves all the behavior into the testable surface and leaves the React glue as a thin wiring layer. This matches `[[pure-helper-extraction-pattern]]` from Phase 7 #7c-2 (Edge-incompatible auth callback extraction) and #8e (Socket.IO server auth.ts extraction) — same insight applied to React.
  - Why `withCredentials:true` instead of cookie sharing via path: SOCKET_PORT=43515 is a separate origin from APP_PORT=43512. The Auth.js session cookie is bound to APP_PORT's origin; for the socket handshake to carry it, the browser needs `withCredentials:true` AND the server needs `cors.credentials:true` (already configured in apps/web/src/server/socket/server.ts:30 from Phase 7 #8e-1). Same-origin cookie sharing was rejected at Phase 7 #8e-1 in favor of separate-port (option B) for Docker `output: standalone` portability. Cross-link to DECISIONS_LOG line 173.
  - Why redirect to /login on session:invalidated instead of inline reauth: the server emits this event when DB security_version no longer matches the JWT version (role change, suspension, password reset). The user's role/permissions/tenant binding may have changed. The only safe response is a fresh Auth.js cycle — `router.push("/login") + router.refresh()` evicts cached authenticated layouts AND forces Auth.js's credentials provider to re-derive `session.user` from the database. Inline reauth would require server round-trips identical to a login flow but with worse UX (stale layout shells visible during refetch).
  - Why no unit test for SocketProvider: Yelli vitest env is `node`, jsdom is NOT installed, @testing-library/react is NOT installed. Adding both would add 2 deps + a config bifurcation (jsdom env per-file directive). This is a Phase 7 N+M ticket of its own, not a #10 scope addition. The Provider is shielded by typecheck + lint + the two pure-helper tests (which cover 100% of the testable behavior). When Phase 7 #11 (presence) ships, the end-to-end browser smoke (Rule 16 Visual QA) will exercise the Provider directly. Adding test infra now would be YAGNI.
  - PreToolUse security_reminder_hook false-positive history: this is the 4th time the hook fires on a non-event-input ci.yml edit (Phase 7 #5, #7c-2, #9, and now #10). The hook fires on file-pattern match (`.github/workflows/*.yml`), not on diff content analysis. Pattern documented in Phase 7 #5 lessons; no new lesson needed.
  - PostToolUse validator false-positive for `async headers()`: fired 3 times on next.config.ts edits. The validator conflates the Next.js config-time `async headers()` function (returns header definitions; has been async since Next.js 13) with the Next.js 16 runtime `headers()` API from `next/headers`. Yelli is on Next.js 15.5.18 stable and the file does not import or call the runtime headers() API. No new lesson — same class as [[proxy-ts-false-positive]].
  - Vercel-plugin auto-injected ~10 skills across Read/Write operations (next-forge, next-cache-components, nextjs, react-best-practices, next-upgrade, turbopack, workflow, deployments-cicd, auth/Clerk). All ignored per [[proxy-ts-false-positive]] precedent. The plugin matches on file-path / import-substring patterns, not on actual codebase configuration. No new lesson needed.
